reviewer: Producer: directed datapath lockstep, honest reachable/structural/cross-slice split
approver: Approver: verified vs core.v RTL + Spike lockstep 2026-06-09
authority: Spike per-commit lockstep PASS (43 commits, dut_commit.trace == spike_commit.trace, independently verified)
owns: core.v DATAPATH integrator LOGIC: forward/bypass select, load-use stall, muldiv busy stall, flush-vs-forward priority, wb_sel/result mux, mem_stall, MEM sign/zero-ext glue. Delta +499 core.v toggles (68.3%->78.5%).

waivers.structural_basis: Pipeline PC registers latch the fetch PC (core.v:291 if_ex_pc<=if_pc; 780/783/905/908 propagation). Instruction PCs are 2-byte aligned (RV32C), so PC bit[0] is constant 0 - cannot toggle.
waivers.stimulus_considered: RV32C + RV32I instruction stream; no legal instruction PC is odd

deferred_cross_slice.basis: PC address-RANGE high bits toggle only when code executes at high addresses — a fetch/PC-management + memory-map placement concern owned by the P16 core IF slice, not P15 datapath logic. P15 closed PC[21:8] via address spread; [31:22] need P16's memory-map/SKU config (MEM_TOP).

Misalign-detect + data-trap signals toggle on a misaligned LW/LH which TRAPS (mcause 4/6); keeping Spike lockstep through the trap needs an mtvec handler = the P17 CSR/trap slice's harness. Datapath detection is correct; closure belongs to P17.
